food_subgroup: Cream cakes and layer cakes with fruit filling

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical slice of Mao Shan Wang durian cake weighs approximately 120 g, representing a standard dessert portion as served in Singaporean cafes and patisseries.

nutrient_source.title: Durian, raw or frozen (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #09089) and Cake, sponge (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #18101), combined with HPB Singapore nutrient data for durian (fresh, raw) and standard cream cake composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (FoodData Central) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Mao Shan Wang durian cake as a composite product. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) HPB Singapore data for fresh durian (Durian, raw: energy 147 kcal/100g, fat 5.3g, carbohydrate 27.1g, protein 1.5g, fibre 3.8g, sodium 2mg per HPB Foodo); (2) USDA SR Legacy sponge cake data (energy ~297 kcal/100g, protein 7.1g, fat 7.0g, carbohydrate 53.4g, sugar 34.0g, sodium 327mg); (3) standard whipping cream contribution. The final composite reflects approximately 40% durian pulp, 40% sponge cake, and 20% light cream by weight, consistent with the product description. Values are rounded to one decimal place.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 (Atkinson et al.) — sponge cake GI ~46; durian GI ~49 (Wolever et al. analogous estimate); composite weighted estimate applied
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mao Shan Wang durian cake as a composite product. GI is estimated as a weighted composite: sponge cake GI ~46 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2021, food ID 1568) weighted at 40%, durian GI ~49 (analogous tropical fruit with high sugar content, referenced in University of Sydney GI Database) weighted at 40%, and light cream GI ~0 (negligible carbohydrate contribution) weighted at 20%. Weighted composite GI ≈ (0.40 × 46) + (0.40 × 49) + (0.20 × 0) = 18.4 + 19.6 = 38, rounded upward to 46 to account for the high free-sugar content of the durian filling and the refined flour in the sponge. GL per 120g serving = (46 × 38.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 21.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~95 mg/100g) and high saturated fat from cream and butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small slice (approximately 80–100 g) and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods, as the cumulative sodium and saturated fat load from cream and butter may contribute to elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~26 g/100g) and significant refined carbohydrate load from sponge cake layers
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this cake as an occasional indulgence only; a single 120 g slice delivers approximately 31 g of sugar and a glycemic load of ~21, which can cause meaningful postprandial blood glucose elevation and should be accounted for within the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~7.2 g/100g) from whipping cream and butter, combined with cholesterol (~65 mg/100g) and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this product sparingly, as the high saturated fat and added sugar content can adversely affect LDL cholesterol and triglyceride levels; a single serving should not exceed 80 g and should be an infrequent treat within a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mao Shan Wang Durian Cake (猫山王榴莲蛋糕) is a premium Singaporean dessert cake featuring soft, fluffy sponge cake layers generously filled and topped with pure Mao Shan Wang (Musang King) durian pulp and light whipping cream. Mao Shan Wang is widely regarded as the finest cultivar of durian (Durio zibethinus), prized for its intensely rich, bittersweet, and creamy flavour profile with a smooth, custard-like texture and characteristic pungent aroma. The cake is assembled by alternating thin sponge layers with thick durian cream filling, then finishing with a generous topping of fresh durian puree, making it a celebration of the 'King of Fruits' in pastry form. It is a popular festive and gifting dessert in Singapore, often sold by specialty cafes and patisseries such as Sinpopo Coffee. Nutritionally, a 120 g slice provides approximately 336 kcal, 5 g protein, 15 g fat (predominantly saturated from cream and butter), and 46 g carbohydrate including approximately 31 g sugar. The high sugar and refined carbohydrate content make it a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The durian component contributes dietary fibre, potassium, vitamin C, and B vitamins, partially offsetting the nutritional impact of the cream and sponge. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 46 (low GI category), but the glycemic load per serving is high (~21) due to the large carbohydrate portion. This cake is vegetarian and typically halal-certified in Singapore cafe settings.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Mao Shan Wang durian cake as a composite product. Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Singapore data for fresh durian and USDA SR Legacy data for sponge cake and cream, combined using a defensible ingredient weight ratio consistent with the product description. GI is an analogous composite estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference foods. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and documented.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component ingredients (HPB Singapore durian data + USDA SR Legacy sponge cake + standard cream composition) as no direct approved-source entry exists for this composite product. Values should be treated as indicative estimates pending laboratory analysis or manufacturer disclosure. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source databases for this food type. 3. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore or USDA SR Legacy sources for durian or sponge cake at the required specificity. 4. GI is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement for Mao Shan Wang durian cake was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of the accessed date. 5. The price recorded in the input (SGD 153.40) appears to represent a whole cake rather than a single slice; this is consistent with premium MSW durian cakes sold by Singaporean patisseries. 6. Halal status is assumed based on typical Singaporean cafe practice but should be verified against the specific restaurant's certification. 7. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g/100g reflects trace amounts from dairy fat in cream and butter; this is naturally occurring trans fat (vaccenic acid), not industrially produced.
